concept.title: Safety Information Before Performing Maintenance
concept.shortdesc: Danger due to electric shocks: Contact with live parts will result in severe injury or

ul.li: Contact with live parts will result in severe injury or death.
Maintenance work must be carried out by authorized, qualified personnel.
Never work when parts are powered, and always make sure that no electricity is supplied for the entire duration of the work.
Before performing any work, always disconnect the electrical power supply at the main or service switch, and lockout-tagout-verify the power. Follow plant procedures.
Use electrically insulated tools.
When performing work “upstream” of the main or service switch, make sure that no electrical power is being supplied.
Place a warning sign at the locked main or service switch to indicate that work is in progress.
The cullet crusher contains moving and heavy parts, and parts under mechanical tension (V-belts, etc.), which may constitute a danger.
Maintenance work be carried out by authorized, qualified personnel.
Before working at any parts, make sure that they are not under mechanical tension.
If necessary, take measures to prevent injury, such as supporting components.
Be careful when replacing heavy machine parts.
Wear the required personal protective equipment.
When maintaining components from suppliers, review the component manufacturer’s documentation, in addition to the information in this document.
During work at the cullet crusher, it is possible that parts and/or transferred materials may fall.
Wear the required personal protective equipment, especially head protection.
Never stand under suspended loads.
Keep unauthorized personnel away from the danger area, and secure the danger area.
Use suitable, tested lifting equipment with appropriate lifting capacity.

li.p: Danger due to materials and substances:

ul.li: Hazardous materials and substances (oil, etc.) are required for operation of the cullet crusher or may arise during operation.
Persons who work with hazardous materials and substances must be appropriately authorized and qualified.
Observe the manufacturer’s warning information when using operating and auxiliary materials.
Wear the required personal protective equipment, especially eye protection, protective clothing and work gloves.
Replace contaminated clothing with clean clothing immediately.

ol.li: Dangers due to gas, smoke, dust:

ul.li: Layers of dust form during the operation of the cullet crusher.
Only perform welding, thermal cutting, or grinding work at the cullet crusher if this has been expressly permitted by an authorized person. This work creates a danger of fire or explosion.
Wear the required personal protective equipment, especially breathing apparatus, eye protection, and work gloves.
Before performing any welding, thermal or grinding work, clean the cullet crusher from dust and combustible materials.
Note the location of the fire alarm and fire extinguishing equipment.
Make sure that there is adequate ventilation due to danger of explosion!
Observe O-I and national regulations when working in confined spaces or containers.
Do not clean the cullet crusher using compressed air or oxygen.
